chapters.title: A pact with what is true
chapters.text:
Each of us arrives with a fingerprint no one else carries. That fact, even if it is hidden from us at first, sets us up for a road of self-discovery full of surprises. All through our lives, we are shaped and reshaped, from our earliest steps led by raw instinct to the point where every convention and expectation becomes an internal mix within us.

Yet there are challenges on this path. As we grow older, we find ourselves caught in a maze of views and ideas that the outside world often presses on us, which can steer us away from our real selves. For example, we often find that social expectations push us to trade our authenticity for approval.

Imagine for a moment that there was a way to untie this knot, that we could take back the story, find our own angle again, and learn to live with a fresh sense of purpose and self-love. We commit to ourselves when we fight for freedom, authenticity, and beauty, because that forces us to look closely, unlearn, and eventually find our true selves again.

Set aside time alone each day to reflect on your values and goals.

Now let us look at Don Miguel Ruiz's fascinating agreements, which are beliefs and internal commitments we can make with ourselves, drawn from the ancient wisdom of the Toltecs. The Toltecs were a culture of skilled warriors and artisans who lived in what is now central Mexico, known for their advanced social and spiritual practices. Curious? Read on. The rest of the book walks through each one in turn, and you can decide which ones to bring into your own life.

chapters.title: Letting go of the symbols
chapters.text:
You no doubt remember that your childhood personality is very different from your current one. That is because, despite what others said, you were the most authentic version of yourself back then.

As you age, society instills its values and norms, shaping your thoughts and expectations. Miguel Ruiz says this is much like domesticating a pet. You learn to please people to get rewards and stay out of trouble. Society lays a reality built on agreements and symbols, creating a web of beliefs that runs your life.

Relationships with others teach you how to behave and how to see the world. You have been trained to speak and think in symbols, to see the world through a particular lens, and to attach meanings to objects. Yet as you grow skilled with these symbols, they begin to rule you.

Over time, we tie our self-worth to what other people think of us. We try to fit the mold that society has built, looking for approval to feel loved. We also keep criticizing ourselves, losing sight of the purity and freedom of our early years, when we were aware of our unique qualities. This process makes us long for who we really are and for what we have lost in the domestication.

Learn to say no to requests that go against your values.

As time passes, our old and new beliefs collide. We start to question what is true and false inside the maze of symbols we have taken in. Soon, we see that our knowledge is just a frame built to make sense of the outside world.

Every sentence you write, every word you speak, and every symbol you use is a small work of art that helps you understand the world and express yourself.

This view can be a complete distortion, a lie you choose to believe, or a fair reflection of the truth. Still, awareness can free you from the chains of domestication, letting you chase new chances and lead a real life.

The first agreement, be impeccable with your words, is a useful reminder to use words carefully and to keep them from being turned against others and yourself.

A 2021 Psychology Today article pointed out that the pressure to show a curated version of ourselves on social media can clash with authenticity.

chapters.title: Calm your mind
chapters.text:
On the road to knowing ourselves better, the second agreement says that every mind is a universe of its own. Do not take anything personally.

This point means we should respect other people's stories and angles and make sure they respect ours in turn. That does not mean we have to agree with them or let them shape how we see ourselves. We are all unique, and our own experiences and beliefs shape how we see things. So our identity is not truly shown by other people's opinions. Real freedom, built on accepting who we are, can only be reached by letting go of the weight of other people's expectations and turning our attention to ourselves. In return, you should not push your expectations onto others and stay aware of your words.

If you feel offended by something outside, the pain comes from how you judge it, not from the fact itself.

It can be hard to learn to live from a place of authenticity and self-worth, but in the end, it comes from learning not to take what others say to heart. This approach makes you stronger against the criticism and judgment of those around you. Remember that everyone is fighting their own battles, and you should not let other people's opinions dictate how you live. It is possible that you read their words wrong from the start, which then makes you feel bad about something that is not real.

With that in mind, you can start living a peaceful, happy, aware life and free yourself from needless pain and suffering.

Choose media that lifts you up and reflects your values.

chapters.title: Avoid assumptions and give your best
chapters.text:
Now let us cover the last two traditional agreements and see how they can lift our lives. The third agreement, do not make assumptions, flags our habit of inventing stories out of thin air. You have probably felt this. You are anxious before a job interview, and your mind keeps running through scenarios where everything goes wrong. In reality, the interview goes well, and you get hired on the spot. Simply put, it is best to skip assumptions, since they only bring tension and upset that leads nowhere.

You may ask how to shake off the habit. It comes down to raising your awareness and challenging the ideas you have already accepted. Of course, you have to practice and commit to see results. You are rewiring your mindset, and that takes time.

The fourth agreement, always do your best, reminds you that the best version of yourself keeps changing. So in any situation, give it your all and do the best you can. Then you will not feel regret or unfair self-criticism, since you can look back and wish you had done or said something different. If you gave your best, the moment is gone, and you can move on.

Notice and celebrate moments when you hold on to your authenticity.

It also helps to change how you see the world and to disprove the limiting ideas you have collected as keepsakes of your life. At first, you may not see these unwanted souvenirs, but reflection will give you the answers you want. In the end, by taking these agreements to heart, you will quickly see that you can shift your reality from one of conflict and limits to a life of full authenticity and freedom.

chapters.title: Skepticism above all
chapters.text:
By now, you are no doubt waiting for the fifth agreement, and it does not disappoint. The fifth agreement tells us to question everything we hear while staying open to new ideas. Be skeptical, but learn to listen. The symbols we see around us are mental constructs and not always the truth. If we learn to listen closely, we can figure out what these symbolic pieces mean and whether they are true or false.

Being skeptical gives you mastery, because you use the power of doubt to read the truth.

This agreement calls for a deeper grasp of human nature and how our beliefs shape how we see the world. All arguments carry the mark of the people making them, so you have to listen without judging. If you do not agree with someone, that is fine, but you have to listen to the end before deciding whether their point is valid for you. Maybe their idea will shift your view, or maybe it will not.

Use mindfulness to stay connected to your current feelings and beliefs.

At some point, though, you have to face the mistaken ideas you took in as a child. It can be a hard process, but it is a needed one. Even if our parents, teachers, and the people in our social circles do not always have the right answers, we learn as kids to believe whatever they say. It becomes second nature. Questioning those ideas lets you build your own view of the world and break free from old social norms and expectations.

chapters.title: The messenger you can grow into
chapters.text:
Have you ever thought about your ideal role as a messenger? What kind of influence do you want to have on those around you? Answering this can be hard if you are surrounded by people who wear masks, or if conformity is prized more than honesty in your circle. While it is good to lift others up, how can you become someone people look up to and follow if you do not have command of yourself?

You can do this by learning to observe your life, breaking free from the bonds of the past, and pulling away from the lies you have built for yourself or for the people you love. It goes without saying that you will need patience and deep reflection. One of the hardest things you will ever do is face your deeply held beliefs.

Three languages shape how people see things from a Toltec view, and they may come up in your search for authenticity. The first is gossip, which everyone knows. In this language, messages get twisted, and everything is built on lies. It is a kind of communication in which you try to confront others about the daily problems you face. The second is the warrior language, which carries the wish to push back against the symbols that have shaped your life. It helps you find your real purpose without anyone blocking your way. The third is the language of truth, a shift that frees you from the bonds of ego and helps you build a full connection with your true self.

With this reflective and speech-shifting practice, you will become a messenger of truths beyond words. Each meeting will give you a moment to think about the message you send into the room. The last word belongs to you. Will you keep promoting old-fashioned ideas of fear and division, or will you be a messenger of love and authenticity? Your answer shapes the kind of adult you are becoming.

Express yourself through art to explore and confirm your identity.

chapters.title: Wrapping up
chapters.text:
As we move from free-spirited, innocent children to adults shaped by social norms and expectations, we often wrestle with the task of lining up our authentic selves with the roles and behaviors society lays on us. Embracing the layers of adulthood gives us a rare chance to forge a path that honors our inner child while skillfully handling social pressures. This road lets us blend wisdom with wonder, so that as we grow, we stay true to ourselves and inspire others to do the same. By holding firm to our core values and dreams, we can build lives that not only meet social standards but also brim with joy and meaning.

Try this. Choose your words with care, and treat yourself the way you treat the people you care for and love most. Take what you are told with a grain of skepticism, since some people make claims without checking their sources. Do not let others use or steer you. Curate your circle by intentionally spending time with people who lift you up and encourage your true self. Small daily acts of honesty will help these agreements take root in your life.
